raw_jd:
Key responsibilities: 

1. Join Wipro as a Scholar Trainee while pursuing an M.Tech degree sponsored by the company.
2. Participate in training programs designed to build technical and professional skills.
3. Work on assigned projects and support business and technology operations.
4. Apply academic knowledge to real-world IT and business challenges.
5. Collaborate with team members to complete project deliverables within deadlines.
6. Attend learning sessions, workshops, and development programs as part of the WILP curriculum.
7. Maintain performance standards set by the organization and academic institution.
8. Communicate effectively with managers, mentors, and project stakeholders.
9. Follow company policies, compliance requirements, and workplace guidelines.
10. Complete assigned tasks while balancing professional responsibilities and higher education commitments.
11. Participate in assessments, reviews, and training evaluations throughout the program.
12. Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives and team objectives.


Salary
1st Year: INR 15,000 stipend per month + INR 488 ESI + INR 75,000 joining bonus.
2nd Year: INR 17,000 stipend per month + INR 533 ESI.
3rd Year: INR 19,000 stipend per month + INR 618 ESI.
4th Year: INR 23,000 stipend per month.

gemini_verdict.experience_reason: The role is a 'Scholar Trainee' program explicitly designed for entry-level candidates pursuing higher education while working.
gemini_verdict.profile_reason: While the candidate is a fresher, this program specifically targets B.Sc/BCA graduates for an M.Tech. The candidate already holds a B.Tech degree, making them overqualified and potentially ineligible for this specific WILP program.
missing_skills: Academic eligibility (B.Tech graduates are often excluded from this specific program)
gemini_verdict.title: Scholar Trainee (Work Integrated Learning Program)
gemini_verdict.summary: A work-integrated learning program where trainees balance full-time employment at Wipro with a company-sponsored M.Tech degree. It is a structured entry-level path intended for specific degree holders to transition into corporate IT roles.
gemini_verdict.match_reason: While the candidate is a fresher, this program specifically targets B.Sc/BCA graduates for an M.Tech. The candidate already holds a B.Tech degree, making them overqualified and potentially ineligible for this specific WILP program.
